title: Themes messages in Eva
wikitext:
[]: Themes/messages in Eva             Quote Post by Shinji-kun » Sat Aug 08, 2009 8:54 am The point of this thread will be to identify and discuss the major, resounding themes in Evangelion in attempts to get at the messages Anno was sending w/ Eva. I'll start off by saying, when I first Saw Eva i thought the lesson was to accept reality. Since joining EvaGeeks i found out Anno also had messages critiscising the otaku culture, but even more addressing the loneliness within all humanity.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sat Aug 08, 2009 9:12 am Hehe, we're discussing that "accept reality" theme now over at Anime Pulse (see the other/non-locked NGE thread). I would reply but I'm too tired ATM to significantly add to this thread.

[]:             Quote Post by Shinji-kun » Sat Aug 08, 2009 9:21 am yeah, higurashi tried to be the Eva yojimbo of anime pulse before you joined. Plagiarism! Ha!

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sat Aug 08, 2009 9:47 am NGE's message boild down to stop watching silly animus and being failure in life and accept the painful bitchy reality

[]:             Quote Post by ath » Sat Aug 08, 2009 12:28 pm Xard wrote: NGE's message boild down to stop watching silly animus and being failure in life and accept the painful bitchy reality

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sat Aug 08, 2009 12:34 pm ath wrote: Xard wrote: NGE's message boild down to stop watching silly animus and being failure in life and accept the painful bitchy reality

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sat Aug 08, 2009 1:19 pm Xard wrote: you can take any philosophical stance or work of art and "reduce" (it's not much reducing instead of brutally whacking pieces of it until it is properly small) it to statement like that.

[]:             Quote Post by ath » Sat Aug 08, 2009 1:52 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: Hamlet = Tragedy about a man who can't make up his mind. ;)

[]:             Quote Post by Soul Fire » Sat Aug 08, 2009 6:53 pm The world is what we make of it?

[]:             Quote Post by schismatics » Sat Aug 08, 2009 8:27 pm Xard wrote: NGE's message boild down to stop watching silly animus and being failure in life and accept the painful bitchy reality

[]:             Quote Post by Shinji-kun » Sat Aug 08, 2009 8:37 pm huh, wont anyone be serious, yojimbo help?

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sat Aug 08, 2009 8:49 pm Shinji-kun wrote: huh, wont anyone be serious, yojimbo help?

[]:             Quote Post by Soul Fire » Sat Aug 08, 2009 9:06 pm I just fail to see purpose in writing some humongous analysis of Evangelion's themes since that has been done hundreds of times before and if for example I wanted to have something relatively new to say I'd have to go very-very-very indepth about show's philosophical, existential side. But that fails to interest me atm.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sat Aug 08, 2009 9:25 pm Soul Fire wrote: I just fail to see purpose in writing some humongous analysis of Evangelion's themes since that has been done hundreds of times before and if for example I wanted to have something relatively new to say I'd have to go very-very-very indepth about show's philosophical, existential side. But that fails to interest me atm.

[]:             Quote Post by Zaque » Sun Aug 09, 2009 3:56 am @Shinji-kun: I think that accepting reality for what it is was important, but the show also tells us to keep in mind what we can do with that reality in front of us, that we can adapt to it, and also overcome things that are perhaps not to our liking, by allowing change to ourselves. That was what the majority of EoTV was about anyway with Shinji's self talk and all. I think EoE had a similar message, just with the extra slap to the face at the end. However, I'm not so sure what you mean by "addressing within all humanity". Did you mean criticism?

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Aug 09, 2009 9:26 am ath wrote: http://tvtropes.org/pmwiki/pmwiki.php/Main/LaconicWiki (disclaimer: as everything on TV Tropes, go read it only if you have *a lot* of time to spare)

Article: Themes messages in Eva
un: I think that accepting reality for what it is was important, but the show also tells us to keep in mind what we can do with that reality in front of us, that we can adapt to it, and also overcome things that are perhaps not to our liking, by allowing change to ourselves. That was what the majority of EoTV was about anyway with Shinji's self talk and all. I think EoE had a similar message, just with the extra slap to the face at the end. However, I'm not so sure what you mean by "addressing within all humanity". Did you mean criticism?
